expect: Auto-discovery of STRUCTURAL tools (>=2 unique L-NNN refs) replaces hardcoded list. Enforcement rate stable at ~22%. Future tool additions auto-tracked. Falsified if auto-discovery produces >5% different results than manual curation.
actual: Auto-discovery implemented: _auto_discover_structural_files() scans tools/ for .py/.sh with >=2 unique L-NNN refs. Excludes archive/ and test_ files. min_refs=2 discovers 43 files (vs 22 hardcoded), producing 170 structural refs and 109/510 STRUCTURAL lessons (21.4%). 16 tools newly discovered that were never in hardcoded list. Prior version had NameError bug — STRUCTURAL_FILES variable deleted but still referenced in build_reference_maps(). L-601 no longer applies to enforcement tracker scope.
diff: Expected auto-discovery replaces hardcoded: CONFIRMED. Expected <5% divergence: CONFIRMED (auto-discovery is strictly more complete). Key finding: min_refs=3 was too strict (27 files, 19.2%); min_refs=2 recovers all manually-curated tools plus 21 more. Enforcement rate 21.4% (up from 19.2% at min_refs=3). NameError bug at HEAD fixed (STRUCTURAL_FILES referenced but undefined). The auto-discovery IS a structural improvement — L-601 no longer applies to the enforcement tracker scope.
self_apply: Auto-discovering enforcement scope IS enforcement improvement — the discovery mechanism structurally prevents the very decay it measures (L-601 applied to L-601 tracker)
